revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''下毛野田道'''（しもつげのたぢ、生年不詳）は[[仁徳天皇|仁徳朝]]の武人、[[下毛野氏]]の祖 [[大荒田別命]]の子、兄に...'
revision.text:
'''下毛野田道'''（しもつげのたぢ、生年不詳）は[[仁徳天皇|仁徳朝]]の武人、[[下毛野氏]]の祖
[[大荒田別命]]の子、兄に[[竹葉瀬]]、子に[[奈良別]]がいる。
仁徳53年[[新羅]]が朝貢を怠ったため、まず兄の[[竹葉瀬]]を詰問使として遣わし、続いて田道が兵を率い遣わされ新羅の兵を撃破した。またその時、四つの邑の住民を捕虜とし連れ帰っている。
仁徳55年[[蝦夷]]が反乱を起すと田道が遣わされるが破れ伊峙水門(いしのみと)で戦死した。 従者は田道が手に巻いていた遺品の玉を妻に与えた。妻は悲しみ縊死した。 後に蝦夷が田道の墓を掘り起こすと、その中から大蛇が現れ次々と噛み付きほとんどの者が死亡したという。
== 関連項目 == * [[毛野氏]]

revision.comment: +stub +category
revision.text:

仁徳53年（[[373年]]）に[[新羅]]が朝貢を怠ったため、まず兄の[[竹葉瀬]]を詰問使として遣わし、続いて田道が兵を率い遣わされ新羅の兵を撃破した。またその時、四つの邑の住民を捕虜とし連れ帰っている。
仁徳55年（[[375年]]）、[[蝦夷]]が反乱を起すと田道が遣わされるが破れ伊峙水門（いしのみと）で戦死した。従者は田道が手に巻いていた遺品の玉を妻に与えた。妻は悲しみ縊死した。後に蝦夷が田道の墓を掘り起こすと、その中から大蛇が現れ次々と噛み付きほとんどの者が死亡したという。

revision.comment: 上毛野・下毛野どちらなのでしょう？
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|2010年6月}} '''下毛野 田道'''（しもつげ の たぢ、生年不詳 - 仁徳55年（[[375年]]））は、[[仁徳天皇|仁徳朝]]の武人、[[下毛野氏]]の祖。[[大荒田別命]]の子で、兄に[[竹葉瀬]]、子に[[奈良別]]がいる。
仁徳53年（[[373年]]）に[[新羅]]が朝貢を怠ったため、まず兄の[[竹葉瀬]]を詰問使として遣わし、続いて田道が兵を率い遣わされ新羅の兵を撃破した。またその時、四つの邑の住民を捕虜とし連れ帰っている。
仁徳55年（[[375年]]）、[[蝦夷]]が反乱を起すと田道が遣わされるが破れ伊峙水門（いしのみと）で戦死した。従者は田道が手に巻いていた遺品の玉を妻に与えた。妻は悲しみ縊死した。後に蝦夷が田道の墓を掘り起こすと、その中から大蛇が現れ次々と噛み付きほとんどの者が死亡したという。
== 関連項目 == * [[毛野氏]]

revision.comment: 下毛野氏の祖の記述削除（後世の系譜で組み込まれることがあるが、日本書紀には記述なし）。
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2010年6月}} [[File:Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg|thumb|220px|田道 ／『前賢故実』より]] '''上毛野 田道'''（かみつけの の たぢ、生年不詳 - 仁徳55年<!--（[[375年]]）：[[Wikipedia:表記ガイド#年月日・時間]]に基づき、西暦の表記はしない。以下同様-->）は、[[仁徳天皇|仁徳朝]]の武人。[[荒田別|大荒田別命]]の子で、兄に[[竹葉瀬]]<!--、子に[[奈良別]]：日本書紀に記述なし-->がいる。
仁徳53年に[[新羅]]が朝貢を怠ったため、まず兄の[[竹葉瀬]]を詰問使として遣わし、続いて田道が兵を率い遣わされ新羅の兵を撃破した。またその時、四つの邑の住民を捕虜とし連れ帰っている。
仁徳55年、[[蝦夷]]が反乱を起すと田道が遣わされるが破れ伊峙水門（いしのみと）で戦死した。従者は田道が手に巻いていた遺品の玉を妻に与えた。妻は悲しみ縊死した。後に蝦夷が田道の墓を掘り起こすと、その中から大蛇が現れ次々と噛み付きほとんどの者が死亡したという。
==参考文献== *『日本書紀』・巻11、 仁徳天皇紀55年の記述
== 関連項目 == * [[毛野氏]]

{{出典の明記|date=2010年6月}} [[File:Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg|thumb|220px|田道 ／『前賢故実』より]] '''上毛野 田道'''（かみつけの の たぢ、生年不詳 - 仁徳55年<!--（[[375年]]）：[[Wikipedia:表記ガイド#年月日・時間]]に基づき、西暦の表記はしない。以下同様-->）は、[[仁徳天皇|仁徳朝]]の武人。[[荒田別|大荒田別命]]の子で、兄に[[竹葉瀬]]<!--、子に[[奈良別]]：日本書紀に記述なし-->がいる。
仁徳53年に[[新羅]]が朝貢を怠ったため、まず兄の[[竹葉瀬]]を詰問使として遣わし、続いて田道が兵を率い遣わされ新羅の兵を撃破した。またその時、四つの邑の住民を捕虜とし連れ帰っている。
仁徳55年、[[蝦夷]]が反乱を起すと田道が遣わされるが破れ伊峙水門（いしのみと）で戦死した。従者は田道が手に巻いていた遺品の玉を妻に与えた。妻は悲しみ縊死した。後に蝦夷が田道の墓を掘り起こすと、その中から大蛇が現れ次々と噛み付きほとんどの者が死亡したという。
==参考文献== *『日本書紀』・巻11、 仁徳天皇紀55年の記述
== 関連項目 == * [[上毛野氏]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かみつけの の たち}} [[Category:生年不明]] [[Category:没年不明]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:上毛野氏|たち]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]]

revision.comment: 修正
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 田道公 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 田道 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[仁徳天皇]] | 氏族 = [[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]・[[下毛野氏|下毛野君]]同族、止美連祖 | 父母 = [[大荒田別命]]<ref name="nakata">[[中田憲信]]『皇胤志』（[[宝賀寿男]]「第2章 皇族系氏族 第8節 毛野氏族 1上毛野君」『古代氏族系譜集成』上巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年、558、559頁。）。</ref> | 兄弟 = [[鹿我別命]]<ref name="nakata"/>、[[現古命]]<ref name="nakata"/>、[[竹葉瀬命]]<ref name="nakata"/>、[[奈良別君]]<ref name="nakata"/>、[[賀表乃眞稚命]]<ref name="nakata"/> | 妻 = 呉女<ref name="nakata"/> | 子 = 特君（持君）<ref name="nakata"/> | 特記事項 = }} [[File:Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg|thumb|220px|田道 ／『[[前賢故実]]』より]] '''田道'''（たぢ、生年不詳 - [[仁徳天皇|仁徳]]55年<!--（[[375年]]）：[[Wikipedia:表記ガイド#年月日・時間]]に基づき、西暦の表記はしない。以下同様-->）は、[[仁徳天皇|仁徳朝]]の武人。田道の氏姓に関して『[[日本書紀]]』には記述されないが、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には'''田道公'''と表記され、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|公]]とされる。
== 概要 == 仁徳53年に[[新羅]]が朝貢を怠ったため、まず兄の[[竹葉瀬]]を詰問使として遣わし、続いて田道が兵を率い遣わされ新羅の兵を撃破した。またその時、四つの邑の住民を捕虜とし連れ帰っている。
仁徳55年、[[蝦夷]]が反乱を起すと田道が遣わされるが敗れ伊峙水門（いしのみと）で戦死した。従者は田道が手に巻いていた遺品の玉を妻に与えた。妻は悲しみ縊死した。後に蝦夷が田道の墓を掘り起こすと、その中から大蛇が現れ次々と噛み付きほとんどの者が死亡したという<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=[[工藤雅樹]]|year=2019|title=蝦夷の古代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|pages=P.71}}</ref>。この墓については、田道の系譜や立地から[[雷神山古墳]]に比定する説がある<ref name="kimikobe">[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm 吉弥侯部姓斑目氏の系譜]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>.
== 系譜 == 『日本書紀』の記述に従えば、[[荒田別|大荒田別命]]の子で[[竹葉瀬]]の弟となるが、実際には[[浮田国造]]の[[鹿我別命]]の子（大荒田別命の甥）と見る説もある<ref name="kimikobe"/>。妻は[[百済|百済国]]止美邑の呉女で、子に止美連の祖・特君（持君）がいる<ref name="nakata"/>。
==参考文献== *『日本書紀』・巻11、 仁徳天皇紀55年の記述
== 脚注== <references/>
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[上毛野氏]]

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 田道公 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 田道 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[仁徳天皇]] | 氏族 = [[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]・[[下毛野氏|下毛野君]]同族、止美連祖 | 父母 = [[大荒田別命]]<ref name="nakata">[[中田憲信]]『皇胤志』（[[宝賀寿男]]「第2章 皇族系氏族 第8節 毛野氏族 1上毛野君」『古代氏族系譜集成』上巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年、558、559頁。）。</ref> | 兄弟 = [[鹿我別命]]<ref name="nakata"/>、[[現古命]]<ref name="nakata"/>、[[竹葉瀬命]]<ref name="nakata"/>、[[奈良別君]]<ref name="nakata"/>、[[賀表乃眞稚命]]<ref name="nakata"/> | 妻 = 呉女<ref name="nakata"/> | 子 = 特君（持君）<ref name="nakata"/> | 特記事項 = }} [[File:Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg|thumb|220px|田道 ／『[[前賢故実]]』より]] '''田道'''（たぢ、生年不詳 - [[仁徳天皇|仁徳]]55年<!--（[[375年]]）：[[Wikipedia:表記ガイド#年月日・時間]]に基づき、西暦の表記はしない。以下同様-->）は、[[仁徳天皇|仁徳朝]]の武人。田道の氏姓に関して『[[日本書紀]]』には記述されないが、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には'''田道公'''と表記され、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|公]]とされる。
== 概要 == 仁徳53年に[[新羅]]が朝貢を怠ったため、まず兄の[[竹葉瀬]]を詰問使として遣わし、続いて田道が兵を率い遣わされ新羅の兵を撃破した。またその時、四つの邑の住民を捕虜とし連れ帰っている。
仁徳55年、[[蝦夷]]が反乱を起すと田道が遣わされるが敗れ伊峙水門（いしのみと）で戦死した。従者は田道が手に巻いていた遺品の玉を妻に与えた。妻は悲しみ縊死した。後に蝦夷が田道の墓を掘り起こすと、その中から大蛇が現れ次々と噛み付きほとんどの者が死亡したという<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=[[工藤雅樹]]|year=2019|title=蝦夷の古代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|pages=P.71}}</ref>。この墓については、田道の系譜や立地から[[雷神山古墳]]に比定する説がある<ref name="kimikobe">[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm 吉弥侯部姓斑目氏の系譜]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>.
== 神格化 == [[蝦夷]]を討伐するために北方へ派遣された田道は、後に[[北海道]]や[[東北]]にて、開拓神として信仰されるようになった<ref> https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref>。 田道を祀る主な神社は以下の通りである。 *[[猿賀神社]] *[[豊平神社]] *[[信濃神社]]
== 伝説 == [[青森県]][[平川市]]の[[猿賀神社]]や、[[宮城県]][[石巻市]]の[[鳥屋崎神社]]には、田道に関する伝承が存在する<ref> http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref> https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>。
== 系譜 == 『日本書紀』の記述に従えば、[[荒田別|大荒田別命]]の子で[[竹葉瀬]]の弟となるが、実際には[[浮田国造]]の[[鹿我別命]]の子（大荒田別命の甥）と見る説もある<ref name="kimikobe"/>。妻は[[百済|百済国]]止美邑の呉女で、子に止美連の祖・特君（持君）がいる<ref name="nakata"/>。
==参考文献== *『日本書紀』・巻11、 仁徳天皇紀55年の記述
== 脚注== <references/>
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[上毛野氏]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かみつけの の たち}} [[Category:生年不明]] [[Category:375年没]] [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:上毛野氏|たち]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:古代日朝関係]] [[Category:日本の紙幣の人物]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 田道公 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 田道 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[仁徳天皇]] | 氏族 = [[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]・[[下毛野氏|下毛野君]]同族、止美連祖 | 父母 = [[大荒田別命]]<ref name="nakata">『日本書紀』</ref> | 兄弟 =[[竹葉瀬命]]<ref name="nakata"/> | 妻 = name="nakata"/> | 特記事項 = }} [[File:Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg|thumb|220px|田道 ／『[[前賢故実]]』より]] '''田道'''（たぢ、生年不詳 - [[仁徳天皇|仁徳]]55年<!--（[[375年]]）：[[Wikipedia:表記ガイド#年月日・時間]]に基づき、西暦の表記はしない。以下同様-->）は、[[仁徳天皇|仁徳朝]]の武人。田道の氏姓に関して『[[日本書紀]]』には記述されないが、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には'''田道公'''と表記され、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|公]]とされる。
== 概要 == 仁徳53年に[[新羅]]が朝貢を怠ったため、まず兄の[[竹葉瀬]]を詰問使として遣わし、続いて田道が兵を率い遣わされ新羅の兵を撃破した。またその時、四つの邑の住民を捕虜とし連れ帰っている。
仁徳55年、[[蝦夷]]が反乱を起すと田道が遣わされるが敗れ伊峙水門（いしのみと）で戦死した。従者は田道が手に巻いていた遺品の玉を妻に与えた。妻は悲しみ縊死した。後に蝦夷が田道の墓を掘り起こすと、その中から大蛇が現れ次々と噛み付きほとんどの者が死亡したという<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=工藤雅樹|authorlink=工藤雅樹|year=2019|title=蝦夷の古代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|page=71}}</ref>。この墓については、田道の系譜や立地から[[雷神山古墳]]に比定する説がある<ref name="kimikobe">[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm 吉弥侯部姓斑目氏の系譜]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>.
== 神格化 == [[蝦夷]]を討伐するために北方へ派遣された田道は、後に[[北海道]]や[[東北]]にて、開拓神として信仰されるようになった<ref> https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref>。 田道を祀る主な神社は以下の通りである。 *[[猿賀神社]] *[[豊平神社]] *[[信濃神社]]
== 伝説 == [[青森県]][[平川市]]の[[猿賀神社]]や、[[宮城県]][[石巻市]]の[[鳥屋神社]]には、田道に関する伝承が存在する<ref> http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref> https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>。
== 系譜 == 『日本書紀』の記述に従えば、[[荒田別|大荒田別命]]の子で[[竹葉瀬]]の弟となる。
==参考文献== *『日本書紀』・巻11、 仁徳天皇紀55年の記述
== 脚注== <references/>
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[上毛野氏]]

== 伝説 == [[青森県]][[平川市]]の[[猿賀神社]]や、[[宮城県]][[石巻市]]の[[鳥屋神社]]には、田道に関する伝承が存在する<ref> http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref> https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>。
== 系譜 == 『日本書紀』の記述に従えば、[[荒田別|大荒田別命]]の子で[[竹葉瀬]]の弟となる。

'''田道'''（たぢ、生年不詳 - [[仁徳天皇|仁徳]]55年<!--（[[375年]]）：[[Wikipedia:表記ガイド#年月日・時間]]に基づき、西暦の表記はしない。以下同様-->）は、[[仁徳天皇|仁徳朝]]の武人。田道の氏姓に関して『[[日本書紀]]』には記述されないが、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には'''田道公'''と表記され、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|公]]とされる。
== 概要 == 仁徳53年に[[新羅]]が朝貢を怠ったため、まず兄の[[竹葉瀬]]を詰問使として遣わし、続いて田道が兵を率い遣わされ新羅の兵を撃破した。またその時、四つの邑の住民を捕虜とし連れ帰っている。
仁徳55年、[[蝦夷]]が反乱を起すと田道が遣わされるが敗れ伊峙水門（いしのみと）で戦死した。従者は田道が手に巻いていた遺品の玉を妻に与えた。妻は悲しみ縊死した。後に蝦夷が田道の墓を掘り起こすと、その中から大蛇が現れ次々と噛み付きほとんどの者が死亡したという<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=工藤雅樹|authorlink=工藤雅樹|year=2019|title=蝦夷の古代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|page=71}}</ref>。
== 神格化 == [[蝦夷]]を討伐するために北方へ派遣された田道は、後に[[北海道]]や[[東北]]にて、開拓神として信仰されるようになった<ref> https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref>。 田道を祀る主な神社は以下の通りである。 *[[猿賀神社]] *[[豊平神社]] *[[信濃神社]]
== 紙幣 == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|国立銀行が発行した兌換紙幣一円券（[[第二国立銀行]]発行）。表面（上段）の右に田道が描かれる。]] 1873年（[[明治]]6年）に発行開始された[[国立銀行紙幣]]の兌換一円券の表面右には、田道の図が描かれた（[[源為朝]]とする説もある）<ref>植村峻 『紙幣肖像の近現代史』吉川弘文館、2015年6月、pp. 66–67 ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>。
== 伝説 == [[青森県]][[平川市]]の[[猿賀神社]]や、[[宮城県]][[石巻市]]の[[鳥屋神社]]には、田道に関する伝承が存在する<ref> http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref> https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>。
== 系譜 == 『日本書紀』の記述に従えば、[[荒田別|大荒田別命]]の子で[[竹葉瀬]]の弟となる。
==参考文献== *『日本書紀』・巻11、 仁徳天皇紀55年の記述
== 脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[上毛野氏]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 田道公 | 画像 = Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg | 画像サイズ = 220px | 画像説明 = [[菊池容斎]]筆『[[前賢故実]] 巻第一』「田道」 | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 田道 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[仁徳天皇]] | 氏族 = [[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]・[[下毛野氏|下毛野君]]同族、止美連祖 | 父母 = [[大荒田別命]]<ref name="nakata">『日本書紀』</ref> | 兄弟 =[[竹葉瀬命]]<ref name="nakata"/> | 妻 = | 特記事項 = }}
'''田道'''（たぢ、生年不詳 - [[仁徳天皇|仁徳]]55年<!--（[[375年]]）：[[Wikipedia:表記ガイド#年月日・時間]]に基づき、西暦の表記はしない。以下同様-->）は、[[仁徳天皇|仁徳朝]]の武人。田道の氏姓に関して『[[日本書紀]]』には記述されないが、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には'''田道公'''と表記され、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|公]]とされる。
== 概要 == 仁徳53年に[[新羅]]が朝貢を怠ったため、まず兄の[[竹葉瀬]]を詰問使として遣わし、続いて田道が兵を率い遣わされ新羅の兵を撃破した。またその時、四つの邑の住民を捕虜とし連れ帰っている。
仁徳55年、[[蝦夷]]が反乱を起すと田道が遣わされるが敗れ伊峙水門（いしのみと）で戦死した。従者は田道が手に巻いていた遺品の玉を妻に与えた。妻は悲しみ縊死した。後に蝦夷が田道の墓を掘り起こすと、その中から大蛇が現れ次々と噛み付きほとんどの者が死亡したという<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=工藤雅樹|authorlink=工藤雅樹|year=2019|title=蝦夷の古代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|page=71}}</ref>。
== 神格化 == [[蝦夷]]を討伐するために北方へ派遣された田道は、後に[[北海道]]や[[東北]]にて、開拓神として信仰されるようになった<ref> https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref>。 田道を祀る主な神社は以下の通りである。 *[[猿賀神社]] *[[豊平神社]] *[[信濃神社]]
== 紙幣 == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|国立銀行が発行した兌換紙幣一円券（[[第二国立銀行]]発行）。表面（上段）の右に田道が描かれる。]] 1873年（[[明治]]6年）に発行開始された[[国立銀行紙幣]]の兌換一円券の表面右には、田道の図が描かれた（[[源為朝]]とする説もある）<ref>植村峻 『紙幣肖像の近現代史』吉川弘文館、2015年6月、pp. 66–67 ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>。一円券
*額面: 壹圓（1円） *表面: 上毛野田道の蝦夷征討（右側）、兵船（左側）（左右両側の図柄を合わせて源為朝（右側）が工藤茂光の軍船（左側）を迎え撃つ光景とする説もある[23]）、頭取・支配人の氏名、兌換文言、公債証書引当文言、記番号 *裏面: 元寇撃退（蒙古襲来）、一圓金貨、法貨表示文言、偽造罰則文言 *印章: 〈表面〉大蔵卿印、出納頭印、記録頭印、頭取印、支配人印 〈裏面〉発行銀行印（割印） *銘板: CONTINENTAL BANKNOTE Co. NEW YORK *記番号色:赤色（英字記号、アラビア数字）［大蔵省記番号］および 緑色（漢字記号、漢数字）［国立銀行記番号］ *寸法: 縦80mm、横190mm *製造枚数: 4,830,130枚[13] *発行: 1873年（明治6年）8月20日 *廃止: 1899年（明治32年）12月9日
表面の図柄については前述の通り2種類の説が存在する。一説は上毛野田道が蝦夷の奇襲攻撃を受ける光景、もう一説は源為朝が工藤茂光の軍船を迎え撃つ光景とされる。なお海岸で大型の弓を構える上毛野田道（または源為朝）の表面右側の図柄については、胴体と比べて頭部が異常に大きく描かれており人物像としては異様な意匠となっている。裏面は元寇の役の戦闘風景が描かれている。
== 伝説 == [[青森県]][[平川市]]の[[猿賀神社]]や、[[宮城県]][[石巻市]]の[[鳥屋神社]]には、田道に関する伝承が存在する<ref> http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref> https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>。
== 系譜 == 『日本書紀』の記述に従えば、[[荒田別|大荒田別命]]の子で[[竹葉瀬]]の弟となる。
==参考文献== *『日本書紀』・巻11、 仁徳天皇紀55年の記述
== 脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[上毛野氏]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かみつけの の たち}} [[Category:生年不明]] [[Category:375年没]] [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:上毛野氏|たち]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:古代日朝関係]] [[Category:日本の紙幣の人物]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 田道公 | 画像 = Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg | 画像サイズ = 220px | 画像説明 = [[菊池容斎]]筆『[[前賢故実]] 巻第一』「田道」 | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 田道将軍 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[仁徳天皇]] | 氏族 = [[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]・[[下毛野氏|下毛野君]]同族、止美連祖 | 父母 = [[大荒田別命]]<ref name="nakata">『日本書紀』</ref> | 兄弟 =[[竹葉瀬命]]<ref name="nakata"/> | 妻 = | 特記事項 = }}
'''田道'''（たぢ、生年不詳 - [[仁徳天皇|仁徳]]55年<!--（[[375年]]）：[[Wikipedia:表記ガイド#年月日・時間]]に基づき、西暦の表記はしない。以下同様-->）は、[[仁徳天皇|仁徳朝]]の武人。田道の氏姓に関して『[[日本書紀]]』には記述されないが、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には'''田道公'''と表記され、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|公]]とされる。
== 概要 == 仁徳53年に[[新羅]]が朝貢を怠ったため、まず兄の[[竹葉瀬]]を詰問使として遣わし、続いて田道が兵を率い遣わされ新羅の兵を撃破した。またその時、四つの邑の住民を捕虜とし連れ帰っている。
仁徳55年、[[蝦夷]]が反乱を起すと田道が遣わされるが敗れ伊峙水門（いしのみと）で戦死した。従者は田道が手に巻いていた遺品の玉を妻に与えた。妻は悲しみ縊死した。後に蝦夷が田道の墓を掘り起こすと、その中から大蛇が現れ次々と噛み付きほとんどの者が死亡したという<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=工藤雅樹|authorlink=工藤雅樹|year=2019|title=蝦夷の古代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|page=71}}</ref>。
== 神格化 == [[蝦夷]]を討伐するために北方へ派遣された田道は、後に[[北海道]]や[[東北]]にて、開拓神として信仰されるようになった<ref> https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref>。 田道を祀る主な神社は以下の通りである。 *[[猿賀神社]] *[[豊平神社]] *[[信濃神社]]
== 紙幣 == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|国立銀行が発行した兌換紙幣一円券（[[第二国立銀行]]発行）。表面（上段）の右に田道が描かれる。]] 1873年（[[明治]]6年）に発行開始された[[国立銀行紙幣]]の兌換一円券の表面右には、田道の図が描かれた（[[源為朝]]とする説もある）<ref>植村峻 『紙幣肖像の近現代史』吉川弘文館、2015年6月、pp. 66–67 ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>。一円券
*額面: 壹圓（1円） *表面: 上毛野田道の蝦夷征討 **（左側）兵船 **（右側）左右両側の図柄を合わせて源為朝（右側）が工藤茂光の軍船（左側）を迎え撃つ光景とする説もある） **（下側）頭取・支配人の氏名、兌換文言、公債証書引当文言、記番号 *裏面: 元寇撃退（蒙古襲来）、一圓金貨、法貨表示文言、偽造罰則文言 *印章: 〈表面〉大蔵卿印、出納頭印、記録頭印、頭取印、支配人印 〈裏面〉発行銀行印（割印） *銘板: CONTINENTAL BANKNOTE Co. NEW YORK *記番号色:赤色（英字記号、アラビア数字）［大蔵省記番号］および 緑色（漢字記号、漢数字）［国立銀行記番号］ *寸法: 縦80mm、横190mm *製造枚数: 4,830,130枚[13] *発行: 1873年（明治6年）8月20日 *廃止: 1899年（明治32年）12月9日
表面の図柄については前述の通り2種類の説が存在する。一説は上毛野田道が蝦夷の奇襲攻撃を受ける光景、もう一説は源為朝が工藤茂光の軍船を迎え撃つ光景とされる。なお海岸で大型の弓を構える上毛野田道（または源為朝）の表面右側の図柄については、胴体と比べて頭部が異常に大きく描かれており人物像としては異様な意匠となっている。裏面は元寇の役の戦闘風景が描かれている。
== 伝説 == [[青森県]][[平川市]]の[[猿賀神社]]や、[[宮城県]][[石巻市]]の[[鳥屋神社]]には、田道に関する伝承が存在する<ref> http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref> https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>。
== 系譜 == 『日本書紀』の記述に従えば、[[荒田別|大荒田別命]]の子で[[竹葉瀬]]の弟となる。
==参考文献== *『日本書紀』・巻11、 仁徳天皇紀55年の記述

== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[上毛野氏]]

'''田道'''（たぢ、生年不詳 - [[仁徳天皇|仁徳]]55年<!--（[[375年]]）：[[Wikipedia:表記ガイド#年月日・時間]]に基づき、西暦の表記はしない。以下同様-->）は、[[仁徳天皇|仁徳朝]]の武人。田道の氏姓に関して『[[日本書紀]]』には記述されないが、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には'''田道公'''と表記され、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|公]]とされる。
== 概要 == 仁徳53年に[[新羅]]が朝貢を怠ったため、まず兄の[[竹葉瀬]]を詰問使として遣わし、続いて田道が兵を率い遣わされ新羅の兵を撃破した。またその時、四つの邑の住民を捕虜とし連れ帰っている。
仁徳55年、[[蝦夷]]が反乱を起すと田道が遣わされるが敗れ伊峙水門（いしのみと）で戦死した。従者は田道が手に巻いていた遺品の玉を妻に与えた。妻は悲しみ縊死した。後に蝦夷が田道の墓を掘り起こすと、その中から大蛇が現れ次々と噛み付きほとんどの者が死亡したという<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=工藤雅樹|authorlink=工藤雅樹|year=2019|title=蝦夷の古代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|page=71}}</ref>。

なお、この紙幣の図柄については2種類の説が存在している。一説は上毛野田道が蝦夷の奇襲攻撃を受ける光景、もう一説は[[源為朝]]が工藤茂光の軍船を迎え撃つ光景とされる。ただし、海岸で大型の弓を構える上毛野田道（または源為朝）の表面右側の図柄については、胴体と比べて頭部が異常に大きく描かれており人物像としては異様な意匠となっている<ref name="uemura"/>。

== 概要 == 仁徳53年に[[新羅]]が朝貢を怠ったため、まず兄の[[竹葉瀬]]を詰問使として遣わし、続いて田道が兵を率い遣わされ新羅の兵を撃破した。またその時、四つの邑の住民を捕虜とし連れ帰っている。
仁徳55年、[[蝦夷]]が反乱を起すと田道が遣わされるが敗れ伊峙水門（いしのみと）で戦死した。従者は田道が手に巻いていた遺品の玉を妻に与えた。妻は悲しみ縊死した。後に蝦夷が田道の墓を掘り起こすと、その中から大蛇が現れ次々と噛み付きほとんどの者が死亡したという<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=工藤雅樹|authorlink=工藤雅樹|year=2019|title=蝦夷の古代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|page=71}}</ref>。
== 神格化 == [[蝦夷]]を討伐するために北方へ派遣された田道は、後に[[北海道]]や[[東北]]にて、開拓神として信仰されるようになった<ref> https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref>。 田道を祀る主な神社は以下の通りである。 *[[猿賀神社]] *[[豊平神社]] *[[信濃神社]]
== 紙幣 == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|国立銀行が発行した兌換紙幣一円券（[[第二国立銀行]]発行）。表面（上段）の右に田道が描かれる。]] {{main|国立銀行紙幣#一円券}} 1873年（[[明治]]6年）に発行開始された[[国立銀行紙幣]]の兌換一円券の表面右には、田道の図が描かれた<ref name="uemura">植村峻 『紙幣肖像の近現代史』吉川弘文館、2015年6月、pp. 66–67 ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>。
なお、この紙幣の図柄については2種類の説が存在している。一説は上毛野田道が蝦夷の奇襲攻撃を受ける光景、もう一説は[[源為朝]]が工藤茂光の軍船を迎え撃つ光景とされる。ただし、海岸で大型の弓を構える上毛野田道（または源為朝）の表面右側の図柄については、胴体と比べて頭部が異常に大きく描かれており人物像としては異様な意匠となっている<ref name="uemura"/>。
== 伝説 == [[青森県]][[平川市]]の[[猿賀神社]]や、[[宮城県]][[石巻市]]の[[鳥屋神社]]には、田道に関する伝承が存在する<ref> http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref> https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>。
== 系譜 == 『日本書紀』の記述に従えば、[[荒田別|大荒田別命]]の子で[[竹葉瀬]]の弟となる。
==参考文献== *『日本書紀』・巻11、 仁徳天皇紀55年の記述
== 脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[上毛野氏]] * [[田道町 (石巻市)]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かみつけの の たち}} [[Category:生年不明]] [[Category:375年没]] [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:上毛野氏|たち]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:古代日朝関係]] [[Category:日本の紙幣の人物]]

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 田道公 | 画像 = Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg | 画像サイズ = 220px | 画像説明 = [[菊池容斎]]筆『[[前賢故実]] 巻第一』「田道」 | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 田道将軍 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[仁徳天皇]] | 氏族 = [[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]・[[下毛野氏|下毛野君]]同族、止美連祖 | 父母 = [[大荒田別命]]<ref name="nakata">『日本書紀』</ref> | 兄弟 =[[竹葉瀬命]]<ref name="nakata"/> | 妻 = | 特記事項 = }}
'''田道'''（たぢ、生年不詳 - [[仁徳天皇|仁徳]]55年<!--（[[375年]]）：[[Wikipedia:表記ガイド#年月日・時間]]に基づき、西暦の表記はしない。以下同様-->）は、[[仁徳天皇|仁徳朝]]の武人。田道の氏姓に関して『[[日本書紀]]』には記述されないが、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には'''田道公'''と表記され、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|公]]とされる。
== 概要 == 仁徳53年に[[新羅]]が朝貢を怠ったため、まず兄の[[竹葉瀬]]を詰問使として遣わし、続いて田道が兵を率い遣わされ新羅の兵を撃破した。またその時、四つの邑の住民を捕虜とし連れ帰っている。
仁徳55年、[[蝦夷]]が反乱を起すと田道が遣わされるが敗れ伊峙水門（いしのみと）で戦死した。従者は田道が手に巻いていた遺品の玉を妻に与えた。妻は悲しみ縊死した。後に蝦夷が田道の墓を掘り起こすと、その中から大蛇が現れ次々と噛み付きほとんどの者が死亡したという<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=工藤雅樹|authorlink=工藤雅樹|year=2019|title=蝦夷の古代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|page=71}}</ref>。
== 神格化 == [[蝦夷]]を討伐するために北方へ派遣された田道は、後に[[北海道]]や[[東北]]にて、開拓神として信仰されるようになった<ref> https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref>。 田道を祀る主な神社は以下の通りである。 *[[猿賀神社]] *[[豊平神社]] *[[信濃神社]]
== 紙幣 == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|国立銀行が発行した兌換紙幣一円券（[[第二国立銀行]]発行）。表面（上段）の右に田道が描かれる。]] {{main|国立銀行紙幣#一円券}} 1873年（[[明治]]6年）に発行開始された[[国立銀行紙幣]]の兌換一円券の表面右には、田道の図が描かれた<ref name="uemura">植村峻 『紙幣肖像の近現代史』吉川弘文館、2015年6月、pp. 66–67 ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>。
なお、この紙幣の図柄については2種類の説が存在している。一説は上毛野田道が蝦夷の奇襲攻撃を受ける光景、もう一説は[[源為朝]]が工藤茂光の軍船を迎え撃つ光景とされる。ただし、海岸で大型の弓を構える上毛野田道（または源為朝）の表面右側の図柄については、胴体と比べて頭部が異常に大きく描かれており人物像としては異様な意匠となっている<ref name="uemura"/>。
== 伝説 == [[青森県]][[平川市]]の[[猿賀神社]]や、[[宮城県]][[石巻市]]の[[鳥屋神社]]には、田道に関する伝承が存在する<ref> http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref> https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>。

==参考文献== *『日本書紀』・巻11、 仁徳天皇紀55年の記述

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Tamichi | image = Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg | image_size = 220px | caption = {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|ja|菊池容斎}}’s depiction of Tamichi in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実}}'' | era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tamichi General | honorific_suffix = | deified_as = | grave = | rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, ancestor of the Tomi no Muraji | parents = {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}<ref name="nakata">''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''</ref> | siblings = {{ill|Takehase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬命}}<ref name="nakata"/> | spouse = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. His clan and title are not described in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', but the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' refers to him as '''Tamichi no Kimi''' (田道公) with the kabane title of ''Kimi''.
== Overview ==
In the 53rd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign, {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} neglected its tribute obligations. Tamichi’s elder brother, {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}, was dispatched as an envoy to investigate. Later, Tamichi led a military force to defeat the Silla troops. During the campaign, he captured and brought back the inhabitants of four settlements.
In the 55th year of Nintoku's reign, Tamichi was sent to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but he was defeated and killed at the Ishinomito waterway. After his death, his attendant gave Tamichi’s keepsake, a jade he had wrapped around his hand, to his wife, who, stricken with grief, took her own life. Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>
== Deification ==
Tamichi, who was sent north to suppress the Emishi, was later venerated as a deity of frontier development in {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} and {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北}}.<ref>https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref> Major shrines dedicated to him include: * {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} * {{ill|Toyohira Shrine|ja|豊平神社}} * {{ill|Shinano Shrine|ja|信濃神社}}
== Banknote Depiction == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|Front and reverse of an early one-yen banknote issued by the {{ill|Second National Bank of Japan|ja|第二国立銀行}}. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|ja|源為朝}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|ja|平川市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|ja|石巻市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町}}
[[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:375 deaths]] [[Category:Mythological figures of Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese people]] [[Category:People of the Kofun period]] [[Category:Kamitsukeno clan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Ancient Japan-Korea relations]] [[Category:Figures depicted on Japanese banknotes]] [[ja:田道]]

ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|ja|源為朝}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>

== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Tamichi | image = Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg | image_size = 220px | caption = {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|ja|菊池容斎}}’s depiction of Tamichi in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実}}'' | era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tamichi General | honorific_suffix = | deified_as = | grave = | rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, ancestor of the Tomi no Muraji | parents = {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}<ref name="nakata">''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''</ref> | siblings = {{ill|Takehase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬命}}<ref name="nakata"/> | spouse = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. His clan and title are not described in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', but the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' refers to him as '''Tamichi no Kimi''' (田道公) with the kabane title of ''Kimi''.
== Overview ==
In the 53rd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign, {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} neglected its tribute obligations. Tamichi’s elder brother, {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}, was dispatched as an envoy to investigate. Later, Tamichi led a military force to defeat the Silla troops. During the campaign, he captured and brought back the inhabitants of four settlements.
In the 55th year of Nintoku's reign, Tamichi was sent to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but he was defeated and killed at the Ishinomito waterway. After his death, his attendant gave Tamichi’s keepsake, a jade he had wrapped around his hand, to his wife, who, stricken with grief, took her own life. Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>
== Deification ==
Tamichi, who was sent north to suppress the Emishi, was later venerated as a deity of frontier development in {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} and {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北}}.<ref>https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref> Major shrines dedicated to him include: * {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} * {{ill|Toyohira Shrine|ja|豊平神社}} * {{ill|Shinano Shrine|ja|信濃神社}}
== Banknote Depiction == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|Front and reverse of an early one-yen banknote issued by the {{ill|Second National Bank of Japan|ja|第二国立銀行}}. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|ja|源為朝}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|ja|平川市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|ja|石巻市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Tamichi | image = Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg | image_size = 220px | caption = {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|ja|菊池容斎}}’s depiction of Tamichi in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実}}'' | era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tamichi General | honorific_suffix = | deified_as = | grave = | rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, ancestor of the Tomi no Muraji | parents = {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}<ref name="nakata">''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''</ref> | siblings = {{ill|Takehase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬命}}<ref name="nakata"/> | spouse = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. His clan and title are not described in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', but the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' refers to him as '''Tamichi no Kimi''' (田道公) with the kabane title of ''Kimi''.
== Overview ==
In the 53rd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign, {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} neglected its tribute obligations. Tamichi’s elder brother, {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}, was dispatched as an envoy to investigate. Later, Tamichi led a military force to defeat the Silla troops. During the campaign, he captured and brought back the inhabitants of four settlements.
In the 55th year of Nintoku's reign, Tamichi was sent to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but he was defeated and killed at the Ishinomito waterway. After his death, his attendant gave Tamichi’s keepsake, a jade he had wrapped around his hand, to his wife, who, stricken with grief, took her own life. Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>
== Deification ==
Tamichi, who was sent north to suppress the Emishi, was later venerated as a deity of frontier development in {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} and {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北}}.<ref>https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref> Major shrines dedicated to him include: * {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} * {{ill|Toyohira Shrine|ja|豊平神社}} * {{ill|Shinano Shrine|ja|信濃神社}}
== Banknote Depiction == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|Front and reverse of an early one-yen banknote issued by the {{ill|Second National Bank of Japan|ja|第二国立銀行}}. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|ja|源為朝}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|ja|平川市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|ja|石巻市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町}}

== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.

== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Tamichi | image = Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg | image_size = 220px | caption = {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|ja|菊池容斎}}’s depiction of Tamichi in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実}}'' | era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tamichi General | honorific_suffix = | deified_as = | grave = | rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, ancestor of the Tomi no Muraji | parents = {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}<ref name="nakata">''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''</ref> | siblings = {{ill|Takehase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬命}}<ref name="nakata"/> | spouse = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. His clan and title are not described in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', but the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' refers to him as '''Tamichi no Kimi''' (田道公) with the kabane title of ''Kimi''.
== Overview ==
In the 53rd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign, {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} neglected its tribute obligations. Tamichi’s elder brother, {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}, was dispatched as an envoy to investigate. Later, Tamichi led a military force to defeat the Silla troops. During the campaign, he captured and brought back the inhabitants of four settlements.
In the 55th year of Nintoku's reign, Tamichi was sent to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but he was defeated and killed at the Ishinomito waterway. After his death, his attendant gave Tamichi’s keepsake, a jade he had wrapped around his hand, to his wife, who, stricken with grief, took her own life. Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>
== Deification ==
Tamichi, who was sent north to suppress the Emishi, was later venerated as a deity of frontier development in {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} and {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北}}.<ref>https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref> Major shrines dedicated to him include: * {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} * {{ill|Toyohira Shrine|ja|豊平神社}} * {{ill|Shinano Shrine|ja|信濃神社}}
== Banknote Depiction == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|Front and reverse of an early one-yen banknote issued by the {{ill|Second National Bank of Japan|ja|第二国立銀行}}. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|ja|源為朝}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|ja|平川市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|ja|石巻市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町}}

== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|ja|平川市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|ja|石巻市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Tamichi | image = Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg | image_size = 220px | caption = {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|ja|菊池容斎}}’s depiction of Tamichi in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実}}'' | era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tamichi General | honorific_suffix = | deified_as = | grave = | rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, ancestor of the Tomi no Muraji | parents = {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}<ref name="nakata">''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''</ref> | siblings = {{ill|Takehase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬命}}<ref name="nakata"/> | spouse = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. His clan and title are not described in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', but the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' refers to him as '''Tamichi no Kimi''' (田道公) with the kabane title of ''Kimi''.
== Overview ==
In the 53rd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign, {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} neglected its tribute obligations. Tamichi’s elder brother, {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}, was dispatched as an envoy to investigate. Later, Tamichi led a military force to defeat the Silla troops. During the campaign, he captured and brought back the inhabitants of four settlements.
In the 55th year of Nintoku's reign, Tamichi was sent to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but he was defeated and killed at the Ishinomito waterway. After his death, his attendant gave Tamichi’s keepsake, a jade he had wrapped around his hand, to his wife, who, stricken with grief, took her own life. Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>
== Deification ==
Tamichi, who was sent north to suppress the Emishi, was later venerated as a deity of frontier development in {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} and {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北}}.<ref>https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref> Major shrines dedicated to him include: * {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} * {{ill|Toyohira Shrine|ja|豊平神社}} * {{ill|Shinano Shrine|ja|信濃神社}}
== Banknote Depiction == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|Front and reverse of an early one-yen banknote issued by the {{ill|Second National Bank of Japan|ja|第二国立銀行}}. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|ja|源為朝}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|ja|平川市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|ja|石巻市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町}}

{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Tamichi | image = Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg | image_size = 220px | caption = {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|ja|菊池容斎}}’s depiction of Tamichi in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実}}'' | era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tamichi General | honorific_suffix = | deified_as = | grave = | rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, ancestor of the Tomi no Muraji | parents = {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}<ref name="nakata">''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''</ref> | siblings = {{ill|Takehase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬命}}<ref name="nakata"/> | spouse = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. His clan and title are not described in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', but the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' refers to him as '''Tamichi no Kimi''' (田道公) with the kabane title of ''Kimi''.
== Overview ==
In the 53rd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign, {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} neglected its tribute obligations. Tamichi’s elder brother, {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}, was dispatched as an envoy to investigate. Later, Tamichi led a military force to defeat the Silla troops. During the campaign, he captured and brought back the inhabitants of four settlements.
In the 55th year of Nintoku's reign, Tamichi was sent to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but he was defeated and killed at the Ishinomito waterway. After his death, his attendant gave Tamichi’s keepsake, a jade he had wrapped around his hand, to his wife, who, stricken with grief, took her own life. Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>
== Deification ==
Tamichi, who was sent north to suppress the Emishi, was later venerated as a deity of frontier development in {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} and {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北}}.<ref>https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref> Major shrines dedicated to him include: * {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} * {{ill|Toyohira Shrine|ja|豊平神社}} * {{ill|Shinano Shrine|ja|信濃神社}}
== Banknote Depiction == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|Front and reverse of an early one-yen banknote issued by the {{ill|Second National Bank of Japan|ja|第二国立銀行}}. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|ja|源為朝}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|ja|平川市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|ja|石巻市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町}}

== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|ja|平川市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|ja|石巻市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Tamichi | image = Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg | image_size = 220px | caption = {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|ja|菊池容斎}}’s depiction of Tamichi in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実}}'' | era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tamichi General | honorific_suffix = | deified_as = | grave = | rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, ancestor of the Tomi no Muraji | parents = {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}<ref name="nakata">''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''</ref> | siblings = {{ill|Takehase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬命}}<ref name="nakata"/> | spouse = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. His clan and title are not described in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', but the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' refers to him as '''Tamichi no Kimi''' (田道公) with the kabane title of ''Kimi''.
== Overview ==
In the 53rd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign, {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} neglected its tribute obligations. Tamichi’s elder brother, {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}, was dispatched as an envoy to investigate. Later, Tamichi led a military force to defeat the Silla troops. During the campaign, he captured and brought back the inhabitants of four settlements.
In the 55th year of Nintoku's reign, Tamichi was sent to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but he was defeated and killed at the Ishinomito waterway. After his death, his attendant gave Tamichi’s keepsake, a jade he had wrapped around his hand, to his wife, who, stricken with grief, took her own life. Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>
== Deification ==
Tamichi, who was sent north to suppress the Emishi, was later venerated as a deity of frontier development in {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} and {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北}}.<ref>https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref> Major shrines dedicated to him include: * {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} * {{ill|Toyohira Shrine|ja|豊平神社}} * {{ill|Shinano Shrine|ja|信濃神社}}
== Banknote Depiction == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|Front and reverse of an early one-yen banknote issued by the {{ill|Second National Bank of Japan|ja|第二国立銀行}}. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|ja|源為朝}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|ja|平川市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|ja|石巻市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町}}

'''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. His clan and title are not described in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', but the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' refers to him as '''Tamichi no Kimi''' (田道公) with the kabane title of ''Kimi''.

In the 55th year of Nintoku's reign, Tamichi was sent to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but he was defeated and killed at the Ishinomito waterway. After his death, his attendant gave Tamichi’s keepsake, a jade he had wrapped around his hand, to his wife, who, stricken with grief, took her own life. Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>

ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Tamichi | image = Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg | image_size = 220px | caption = {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|ja|菊池容斎}}’s depiction of Tamichi in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実}}'' | era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tamichi General | honorific_suffix = | deified_as = | grave = | rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, ancestor of the Tomi no Muraji | parents = {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}<ref name="nakata">''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''</ref> | siblings = {{ill|Takehase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬命}}<ref name="nakata"/> | spouse = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. His clan and title are not described in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', but the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' refers to him as '''Tamichi no Kimi''' (田道公) with the kabane title of ''Kimi''.
== Overview ==
In the 53rd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign, {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} neglected its tribute obligations. Tamichi’s elder brother, {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}, was dispatched as an envoy to investigate. Later, Tamichi led a military force to defeat the Silla troops. During the campaign, he captured and brought back the inhabitants of four settlements.
In the 55th year of Nintoku's reign, Tamichi was sent to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but he was defeated and killed at the Ishinomito waterway. After his death, his attendant gave Tamichi’s keepsake, a jade he had wrapped around his hand, to his wife, who, stricken with grief, took her own life. Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>
== Deification ==
Tamichi, who was sent north to suppress the Emishi, was later venerated as a deity of frontier development in {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} and {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北}}.<ref>https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref> Major shrines dedicated to him include: * {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} * {{ill|Toyohira Shrine|ja|豊平神社}} * {{ill|Shinano Shrine|ja|信濃神社}}
== Banknote Depiction == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|Front and reverse of an early one-yen banknote issued by the {{ill|Second National Bank of Japan|ja|第二国立銀行}}. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|ja|源為朝}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|ja|平川市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|ja|石巻市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町}}

'''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|ja|源為朝}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Tamichi | image = Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg | image_size = 220px | caption = {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|ja|菊池容斎}}’s depiction of Tamichi in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実}}'' | era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tamichi General | honorific_suffix = | deified_as = | grave = | rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, ancestor of the Tomi no Muraji | parents = {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}<ref name="nakata">''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''</ref> | siblings = {{ill|Takehase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬命}}<ref name="nakata"/> | spouse = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. His clan and title are not described in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', but the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' refers to him as '''Tamichi no Kimi''' (田道公) with the kabane title of ''Kimi''.
== Overview ==
In the 53rd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign, {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} neglected its tribute obligations. Tamichi’s elder brother, {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}, was dispatched as an envoy to investigate. Later, Tamichi led a military force to defeat the Silla troops. During the campaign, he captured and brought back the inhabitants of four settlements.
In the 55th year of Nintoku's reign, Tamichi was sent to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but he was defeated and killed at the Ishinomito waterway. After his death, his attendant gave Tamichi’s keepsake, a jade he had wrapped around his hand, to his wife, who, stricken with grief, took her own life. Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>
== Deification ==
Tamichi, who was sent north to suppress the Emishi, was later venerated as a deity of frontier development in {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} and {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北}}.<ref>https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref> Major shrines dedicated to him include: * {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} * {{ill|Toyohira Shrine|ja|豊平神社}} * {{ill|Shinano Shrine|ja|信濃神社}}
== Banknote Depiction == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|Front and reverse of an early one-yen banknote issued by the {{ill|Second National Bank of Japan|ja|第二国立銀行}}. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|ja|源為朝}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|ja|平川市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|ja|石巻市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Tamichi | image = Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg | image_size = 220px | caption = {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|ja|菊池容斎}}’s depiction of Tamichi in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実}}'' | era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tamichi General | honorific_suffix = | deified_as = | grave = | rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, ancestor of the Tomi no Muraji | parents = {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}<ref name="nakata">''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''</ref> | siblings = {{ill|Takehase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬命}}<ref name="nakata"/> | spouse = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. His clan and title are not described in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', but the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' refers to him as '''Tamichi no Kimi''' (田道公) with the kabane title of ''Kimi''.
== Overview ==
In the 53rd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign, {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} neglected its tribute obligations. Tamichi’s elder brother, {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}, was dispatched as an envoy to investigate. Later, Tamichi led a military force to defeat the Silla troops. During the campaign, he captured and brought back the inhabitants of four settlements.
In the 55th year of Nintoku's reign, Tamichi was sent to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but he was defeated and killed at the Ishinomito waterway. After his death, his attendant gave Tamichi’s keepsake, a jade he had wrapped around his hand, to his wife, who, stricken with grief, took her own life. Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>
== Deification ==
Tamichi, who was sent north to suppress the Emishi, was later venerated as a deity of frontier development in {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} and {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北}}.<ref>https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref> Major shrines dedicated to him include: * {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} * {{ill|Toyohira Shrine|ja|豊平神社}} * {{ill|Shinano Shrine|ja|信濃神社}}
== Banknote Depiction == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|Front and reverse of an early one-yen banknote issued by the {{ill|Second National Bank of Japan|ja|第二国立銀行}}. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|ja|源為朝}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|ja|平川市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|ja|石巻市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Tamichi | image = Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg | image_size = 220px | caption = {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|ja|菊池容斎}}’s depiction of Tamichi in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実}}'' | era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tamichi General | honorific_suffix = | deified_as = | grave = | rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, ancestor of the Tomi no Muraji | parents = {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}<ref name="nakata">''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''</ref> | siblings = {{ill|Takehase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬命}}<ref name="nakata"/> | spouse = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. His clan and title are not described in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', but the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' refers to him as '''Tamichi no Kimi''' (田道公) with the kabane title of ''Kimi''.
== Overview ==
In the 53rd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign, {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} neglected its tribute obligations. Tamichi’s elder brother, {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}, was dispatched as an envoy to investigate. Later, Tamichi led a military force to defeat the Silla troops. During the campaign, he captured and brought back the inhabitants of four settlements.
In the 55th year of Nintoku's reign, Tamichi was sent to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but he was defeated and killed at the Ishinomito waterway. After his death, his attendant gave Tamichi’s keepsake, a jade he had wrapped around his hand, to his wife, who, stricken with grief, took her own life. Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>
== Deification ==
Tamichi, who was sent north to suppress the Emishi, was later venerated as a deity of frontier development in {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} and {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北}}.<ref>https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref> Major shrines dedicated to him include: * {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} * {{ill|Toyohira Shrine|ja|豊平神社}} * {{ill|Shinano Shrine|ja|信濃神社}}
== Banknote Depiction == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|Front and reverse of an early one-yen banknote issued by the {{ill|Second National Bank of Japan|ja|第二国立銀行}}. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|ja|源為朝}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|ja|平川市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|ja|石巻市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町}}

{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Tamichi | image = Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg | image_size = 220px | caption = {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|ja|菊池容斎}}’s depiction of Tamichi in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実}}'' | era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tamichi General | honorific_suffix = | deified_as = | grave = | rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, ancestor of the Tomi no Muraji | parents = {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}<ref name="nakata">''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''</ref> | siblings = {{ill|Takehase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬命}}<ref name="nakata"/> | spouse = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. His clan and title are not described in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', but the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' refers to him as '''Tamichi no Kimi''' (田道公) with the kabane title of ''Kimi''.
== Overview ==
In the 53rd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign, {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} neglected its tribute obligations. Tamichi’s elder brother, {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}, was dispatched as an envoy to investigate. Later, Tamichi led a military force to defeat the Silla troops. During the campaign, he captured and brought back the inhabitants of four settlements.
In the 55th year of Nintoku's reign, Tamichi was sent to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but he was defeated and killed at the Ishinomito waterway. After his death, his attendant gave Tamichi’s keepsake, a jade he had wrapped around his hand, to his wife, who, stricken with grief, took her own life. Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>
== Deification ==
Tamichi, who was sent north to suppress the Emishi, was later venerated as a deity of frontier development in {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} and {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北}}.<ref>https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref> Major shrines dedicated to him include: * {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} * {{ill|Toyohira Shrine|ja|豊平神社}} * {{ill|Shinano Shrine|ja|信濃神社}}
== Banknote Depiction == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|Front and reverse of an early one-yen banknote issued by the {{ill|Second National Bank of Japan|ja|第二国立銀行}}. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|ja|源為朝}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|ja|平川市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|ja|石巻市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Tamichi | image = Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg | image_size = 220px | caption = {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|ja|菊池容斎}}’s depiction of Tamichi in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実}}'' | era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tamichi General | honorific_suffix = | deified_as = | grave = | rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, ancestor of the Tomi no Muraji | parents = {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}<ref name="nakata">''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''</ref> | siblings = {{ill|Takehase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬命}}<ref name="nakata"/> | spouse = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. His clan and title are not described in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', but the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' refers to him as '''Tamichi no Kimi''' (田道公) with the kabane title of ''Kimi''.
== Overview ==
In the 53rd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign, {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} neglected its tribute obligations. Tamichi’s elder brother, {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}, was dispatched as an envoy to investigate. Later, Tamichi led a military force to defeat the Silla troops. During the campaign, he captured and brought back the inhabitants of four settlements.
In the 55th year of Nintoku's reign, Tamichi was sent to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but he was defeated and killed at the Ishinomito waterway. After his death, his attendant gave Tamichi’s keepsake, a jade he had wrapped around his hand, to his wife, who, stricken with grief, took her own life. Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>
== Deification ==
Tamichi, who was sent north to suppress the Emishi, was later venerated as a deity of frontier development in {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} and {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北}}.<ref>https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref> Major shrines dedicated to him include: * {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} * {{ill|Toyohira Shrine|ja|豊平神社}} * {{ill|Shinano Shrine|ja|信濃神社}}
== Banknote Depiction == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|Front and reverse of an early one-yen banknote issued by the {{ill|Second National Bank of Japan|ja|第二国立銀行}}. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|ja|源為朝}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|ja|平川市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|ja|石巻市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町}}

'''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. His clan and title are not described in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', but the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' refers to him as '''Tamichi no Kimi''' (田道公) with the kabane title of ''Kimi''.
== Overview ==
In the 53rd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign, {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} neglected its tribute obligations. Tamichi’s elder brother, {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}, was dispatched as an envoy to investigate. Later, Tamichi led a military force to defeat the Silla troops. During the campaign, he captured and brought back the inhabitants of four settlements.
In the 55th year of Nintoku's reign, Tamichi was sent to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but he was defeated and killed at the Ishinomito waterway. After his death, his attendant gave Tamichi’s keepsake, a jade he had wrapped around his hand, to his wife, who, stricken with grief, took her own life. Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>
== Deification ==
Tamichi, who was sent north to suppress the Emishi, was later venerated as a deity of frontier development in {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} and {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北}}.<ref>https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref> Major shrines dedicated to him include: * {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} * {{ill|Toyohira Shrine|ja|豊平神社}} * {{ill|Shinano Shrine|ja|信濃神社}}
== Banknote Depiction == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|Front and reverse of an early one-yen banknote issued by the {{ill|Second National Bank of Japan|ja|第二国立銀行}}. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|ja|源為朝}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruga Shrine|ja|猿賀神社}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|ja|平川市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|ja|石巻市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町}}
