Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>
== Deification ==
Tamichi, who was sent north to suppress the Emishi, was later venerated as a deity of frontier development in {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}} and {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東北地方|1=Tōhoku region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129465}}.<ref>https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref> Major shrines dedicated to him include: * {{ill|Saruka Shrine|en|Saruka Shrine|WD=Q3473746|lt=Saruga Shrine}} * {{ill|Toyohira Shrine|ja|豊平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohira Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Shrine|ja|信濃神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Banknote Depiction == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|Front and reverse of an early one-yen banknote issued by the {{ill|Second National Bank of Japan|ja|第二国立銀行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second National Bank of Japan|1=Second National Bank of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602278}}. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|en|Minamoto no Tametomo|WD=Q1339356|lt=Minamoto no Tametomo}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruka Shrine|en|Saruka Shrine|WD=Q3473746|lt=Saruga Shrine}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|en|Hirakawa, Aomori|WD=Q695930|lt=Hirakawa, Aomori}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|en|Ishinomaki|WD=Q270814|lt=Ishinomaki}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase|qq=}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka period people|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamichi Town|1=Tamichi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131983826}}

Shoki}}''</ref> | siblings = {{ill|Takehase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竹葉瀬|1=Takahase|comment=changed title based on '''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. His clan and title are not described in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', but the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' refers to him as '''Tamichi no Kimi''' (田道公) with the kabane title of ''Kimi''.
== Overview ==
In the 53rd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign, {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}} neglected its tribute obligations. Tamichi’s elder brother, {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase|qq=}}, was dispatched as an envoy to investigate. Later, Tamichi led a military force to defeat the Silla troops. During the campaign, he captured and brought back the inhabitants of four settlements.
In the 55th year of Nintoku's reign, Tamichi was sent to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}}, but he was defeated and killed at the Ishinomito waterway. After his death, his attendant gave Tamichi’s keepsake, a jade he had wrapped around his hand, to his wife, who, stricken with grief, took her own life. Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>
== Deification ==
Tamichi, who was sent north to suppress the Emishi, was later venerated as a deity of frontier development in {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}} and {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東北地方|1=Tōhoku region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129465}}.<ref>https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref> Major shrines dedicated to him include: * {{ill|Saruka Shrine|en|Saruka Shrine|WD=Q3473746|lt=Saruga Shrine}} * {{ill|Toyohira Shrine|ja|豊平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohira Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Shrine|ja|信濃神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Banknote Depiction == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|Front and reverse of an early one-yen banknote issued by the {{ill|Second National Bank of Japan|ja|第二国立銀行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second National Bank of Japan|1=Second National Bank of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602278}}. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|en|Minamoto no Tametomo|WD=Q1339356|lt=Minamoto no Tametomo}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruka Shrine|en|Saruka Shrine|WD=Q3473746|lt=Saruga Shrine}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|en|Hirakawa, Aomori|WD=Q695930|lt=Hirakawa, Aomori}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|en|Ishinomaki|WD=Q270814|lt=Ishinomaki}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase|qq=}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamitsukeno no Tamichi]][[ja:上毛野田道]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamitsukeno no Tamichi]][[ja:上毛野田道]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamitsukeno no Tamichi]][[ja:上毛野田道]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kamitsukeno no Tamichi]][[ja:上毛野田道]]
{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Tamichi | image = Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg | image_size = 220px | caption = {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|en|Kikuchi Yōsai|WD=Q700019|lt=Kikuchi Yōsai}}’s depiction of Tamichi in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|WD=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu}}'' | era = {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tamichi General | honorific_suffix = | deified_as = | grave = | Rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, ancestor of the Tomi no Muraji | parents = {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}}<ref name="nakata">''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}''</ref> | siblings = {{ill|Takehase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竹葉瀬|1=Takahase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209207}}<ref name="nakata"/> | spouse = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. His clan and title are not described in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', but the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' refers to him as '''Tamichi no Kimi''' (田道公) with the kabane title of ''Kimi''.
== Overview ==
In the 53rd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign, {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}} neglected its tribute obligations. Tamichi’s elder brother, {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase|qq=}}, was dispatched as an envoy to investigate. Later, Tamichi led a military force to defeat the Silla troops. During the campaign, he captured and brought back the inhabitants of four settlements.
In the 55th year of Nintoku's reign, Tamichi was sent to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}}, but he was defeated and killed at the Ishinomito waterway. After his death, his attendant gave Tamichi’s keepsake, a jade he had wrapped around his hand, to his wife, who, stricken with grief, took her own life. Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>
== Deification ==
Tamichi, who was sent north to suppress the Emishi, was later venerated as a deity of frontier development in {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}} and {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東北地方|1=Tōhoku region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129465}}.<ref>https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref> Major shrines dedicated to him include: * {{ill|Saruka Shrine|en|Saruka Shrine|WD=Q3473746|lt=Saruga Shrine}} * {{ill|Toyohira Shrine|ja|豊平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohira Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Shrine|ja|信濃神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Banknote Depiction == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|Front and reverse of an early one-yen banknote issued by the {{ill|Second National Bank of Japan|ja|第二国立銀行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second National Bank of Japan|1=Second National Bank of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602278}}. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|en|Minamoto no Tametomo|WD=Q1339356|lt=Minamoto no Tametomo}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruka Shrine|en|Saruka Shrine|WD=Q3473746|lt=Saruga Shrine}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|en|Hirakawa, Aomori|WD=Q695930|lt=Hirakawa, Aomori}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|en|Ishinomaki|WD=Q270814|lt=Ishinomaki}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase|qq=}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka period people|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamichi Town|1=Tamichi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131983826}}
<!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--><!--Kamitsukeno no Tamichi--> [[ja:上毛野田道]] {{translated page|ja|上毛野田道|version=102867989|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11359475}} [[Category:375 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Japan–Korea Relations in Antiquity]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]]

== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruka Shrine|en|Saruka Shrine|WD=Q3473746|lt=Saruga Shrine}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|en|Hirakawa, Aomori|WD=Q695930|lt=Hirakawa, Aomori}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|en|Ishinomaki|WD=Q270814|lt=Ishinomaki}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase|qq=}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka period people|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamichi Town|1=Tamichi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131983826}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamitsukeno no Tamichi]][[ja:上毛野田道]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamitsukeno no Tamichi]][[ja:上毛野田道]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamitsukeno no Tamichi]][[ja:上毛野田道]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kamitsukeno no Tamichi]][[ja:上毛野田道]]
{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Tamichi | image = Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg | image_size = 220px | caption = {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|en|Kikuchi Yōsai|WD=Q700019|lt=Kikuchi Yōsai|1=Kikuchi Yōsai}}’s depiction of Tamichi in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|WD=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu|1=Zenken Kojitsu}}'' | era = {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tamichi General | honorific_suffix = | deified_as = | grave = | Rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, ancestor of the Tomi no Muraji | parents = {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}}<ref name="nakata">''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}''</ref> | siblings = {{ill|Takehase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竹葉瀬|1=Takehase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209207}}<ref name="nakata"/> | spouse = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. His clan and title are not described in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', but the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' refers to him as '''Tamichi no Kimi''' (田道公) with the kabane title of ''Kimi''.
== Overview ==
In the 53rd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign, {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}} neglected its tribute obligations. Tamichi’s elder brother, {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase|qq=}}, was dispatched as an envoy to investigate. Later, Tamichi led a military force to defeat the Silla troops. During the campaign, he captured and brought back the inhabitants of four settlements.
In the 55th year of Nintoku's reign, Tamichi was sent to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}, but he was defeated and killed at the Ishinomito waterway. After his death, his attendant gave Tamichi’s keepsake, a jade he had wrapped around his hand, to his wife, who, stricken with grief, took her own life. Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>
== Deification ==
Tamichi, who was sent north to suppress the Emishi, was later venerated as a deity of frontier development in {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}} and {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東北地方|1=Tōhoku region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129465}}.<ref>https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref> Major shrines dedicated to him include: * {{ill|Saruka Shrine|en|Saruka Shrine|WD=Q3473746|lt=Saruga Shrine|1=Saruka Shrine}} * {{ill|Toyohira Shrine|ja|豊平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohira Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Shrine|ja|信濃神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Banknote Depiction == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|Front and reverse of an early one-yen banknote issued by the {{ill|Second National Bank of Japan|ja|第二国立銀行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second National Bank of Japan|1=Second National Bank of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602278}}. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|en|Minamoto no Tametomo|WD=Q1339356|lt=Minamoto no Tametomo|1=Minamoto no Tametomo}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruka Shrine|en|Saruka Shrine|WD=Q3473746|lt=Saruga Shrine|1=Saruka Shrine}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|en|Hirakawa, Aomori|WD=Q695930|lt=Hirakawa, Aomori|1=Hirakawa, Aomori}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|en|Ishinomaki|WD=Q270814|lt=Ishinomaki|1=Ishinomaki}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase|qq=}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka period people|1=list of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamichi Town|1=Q131983826|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131983826}}

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Tamichi | image = Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg | image_size = 220px | caption = {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|en|Kikuchi Yōsai|WD=Q700019|lt=Kikuchi Yōsai|1=Kikuchi Yōsai}}’s depiction of Tamichi in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|WD=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu|1=Zenken Kojitsu}}'' | era = {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tamichi General | honorific_suffix = | deified_as = | grave = | Rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, ancestor of the Tomi no Muraji | parents = {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}}<ref name="nakata">''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}''</ref> | siblings = {{ill|Takehase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竹葉瀬|1=Takehase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209207}}<ref name="nakata"/> | spouse = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. His clan and title are not described in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', but the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' refers to him as '''Tamichi no Kimi''' (田道公) with the kabane title of ''Kimi''.
== Overview ==
In the 53rd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign, {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}} neglected its tribute obligations. Tamichi’s elder brother, {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase|qq=}}, was dispatched as an envoy to investigate. Later, Tamichi led a military force to defeat the Silla troops. During the campaign, he captured and brought back the inhabitants of four settlements.
In the 55th year of Nintoku's reign, Tamichi was sent to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}, but he was defeated and killed at the Ishinomito waterway. After his death, his attendant gave Tamichi’s keepsake, a jade he had wrapped around his hand, to his wife, who, stricken with grief, took her own life. Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>
== Deification ==
Tamichi, who was sent north to suppress the Emishi, was later venerated as a deity of frontier development in {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}} and {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東北地方|1=Tōhoku region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129465}}.<ref>https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref> Major shrines dedicated to him include: * {{ill|Saruka Shrine|en|Saruka Shrine|WD=Q3473746|lt=Saruga Shrine|1=Saruka Shrine}} * {{ill|Toyohira Shrine|ja|豊平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohira Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Shrine|ja|信濃神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Banknote Depiction == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|Front and reverse of an early one-yen banknote issued by the {{ill|Second National Bank of Japan|ja|第二国立銀行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second National Bank of Japan|1=Second National Bank of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602278}}. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|en|Minamoto no Tametomo|WD=Q1339356|lt=Minamoto no Tametomo|1=Minamoto no Tametomo}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruka Shrine|en|Saruka Shrine|WD=Q3473746|lt=Saruga Shrine|1=Saruka Shrine}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|en|Hirakawa, Aomori|WD=Q695930|lt=Hirakawa, Aomori|1=Hirakawa, Aomori}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|en|Ishinomaki|WD=Q270814|lt=Ishinomaki|1=Ishinomaki}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase|qq=}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka period people|1=list of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamichi Town|1=Q131983826|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131983826}}

The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|en|Minamoto no Tametomo|WD=Q1339356|lt=Minamoto no Tametomo|1=Minamoto no Tametomo}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruka Shrine|en|Saruka Shrine|WD=Q3473746|lt=Saruga Shrine|1=Saruka Shrine}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|en|Hirakawa, Aomori|WD=Q695930|lt=Hirakawa, Aomori|1=Hirakawa, Aomori}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|en|Ishinomaki|WD=Q270814|lt=Ishinomaki|1=Ishinomaki}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase|qq=}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka period people|1=list of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamichi Town|1=Q131983826|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131983826}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamitsukeno no Tamichi]][[ja:上毛野田道]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamitsukeno no Tamichi]][[ja:上毛野田道]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamitsukeno no Tamichi]][[ja:上毛野田道]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kamitsukeno no Tamichi]][[ja:上毛野田道]]
{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Tamichi | image = Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg | image_size = 220px | caption = {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|en|Kikuchi Yōsai|qid=Q700019|lt=Kikuchi Yōsai|1=Kikuchi Yōsai}}’s depiction of Tamichi in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|qid=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu|1=Zenken Kojitsu}}'' | era = {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tamichi General | honorific_suffix = | deified_as = | grave = | Rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, ancestor of the Tomi no Muraji | parents = {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}}<ref name="nakata">''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}''</ref> | siblings = {{ill|Takehase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竹葉瀬|1=Takehase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17209207}}<ref name="nakata"/> | spouse = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. His clan and title are not described in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', but the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' refers to him as '''Tamichi no Kimi''' (田道公) with the kabane title of ''Kimi''.
== Overview ==
In the 53rd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign, {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|qid=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}} neglected its tribute obligations. Tamichi’s elder brother, {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase|qq=}}, was dispatched as an envoy to investigate. Later, Tamichi led a military force to defeat the Silla troops. During the campaign, he captured and brought back the inhabitants of four settlements.
In the 55th year of Nintoku's reign, Tamichi was sent to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|qid=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}, but he was defeated and killed at the Ishinomito waterway. After his death, his attendant gave Tamichi’s keepsake, a jade he had wrapped around his hand, to his wife, who, stricken with grief, took her own life. Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346755}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>
== Deification ==
Tamichi, who was sent north to suppress the Emishi, was later venerated as a deity of frontier development in {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|qid=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}} and {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東北地方|1=Tōhoku region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q129465}}.<ref>https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref> Major shrines dedicated to him include: * {{ill|Saruka Shrine|en|Saruka Shrine|qid=Q3473746|lt=Saruga Shrine|1=Saruka Shrine}} * {{ill|Toyohira Shrine|ja|豊平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohira Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Shrine|ja|信濃神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Banknote Depiction == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|Front and reverse of an early one-yen banknote issued by the {{ill|Second National Bank of Japan|ja|第二国立銀行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second National Bank of Japan|1=Second National Bank of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11602278}}. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|en|Minamoto no Tametomo|qid=Q1339356|lt=Minamoto no Tametomo|1=Minamoto no Tametomo}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruka Shrine|en|Saruka Shrine|qid=Q3473746|lt=Saruga Shrine|1=Saruka Shrine}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|en|Hirakawa, Aomori|qid=Q695930|lt=Hirakawa, Aomori|1=Hirakawa, Aomori}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|qid=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|en|Ishinomaki|qid=Q270814|lt=Ishinomaki|1=Ishinomaki}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|qid=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase|qq=}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period people|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka period people|1=list of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Tamichi Town|ja|田道町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamichi Town|1=Q131983826|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q131983826}}

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamitsukeno no Tamichi | image = Kamitsukenu-Tamichi.jpg | image_size = 220px | caption = {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|en|Kikuchi Yōsai|qid=Q700019|lt=Kikuchi Yōsai|1=Kikuchi Yōsai}}’s depiction of Tamichi in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|qid=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu|1=Zenken Kojitsu}}'' | era = {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tamichi General | honorific_suffix = | deified_as = | grave = | Rank = | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} | clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, ancestor of the Tomi no Muraji | parents = {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}}<ref name="nakata">''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}''</ref> | siblings = {{ill|Takehase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竹葉瀬|1=Takehase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17209207}}<ref name="nakata"/> | spouse = | notable_works = }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Tamichi''' (田道, birth date unknown - 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}’s reign) was a warrior of the Nintoku court. His clan and title are not described in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', but the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' refers to him as '''Tamichi no Kimi''' (田道公) with the kabane title of ''Kimi''.
== Overview ==
In the 53rd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign, {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|qid=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}} neglected its tribute obligations. Tamichi’s elder brother, {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase|qq=}}, was dispatched as an envoy to investigate. Later, Tamichi led a military force to defeat the Silla troops. During the campaign, he captured and brought back the inhabitants of four settlements.
In the 55th year of Nintoku's reign, Tamichi was sent to suppress a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|qid=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}, but he was defeated and killed at the Ishinomito waterway. After his death, his attendant gave Tamichi’s keepsake, a jade he had wrapped around his hand, to his wife, who, stricken with grief, took her own life. Later, when the Emishi desecrated Tamichi’s grave, a giant serpent emerged and attacked them, causing many fatalities.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kudō Masaki|title=The Ancient History of the Emishi|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346755}}|year=2019|page=71}}</ref>
== Deification ==
Tamichi, who was sent north to suppress the Emishi, was later venerated as a deity of frontier development in {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|qid=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}} and {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東北地方|1=Tōhoku region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q129465}}.<ref>https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/</ref> Major shrines dedicated to him include: * {{ill|Saruka Shrine|en|Saruka Shrine|qid=Q3473746|lt=Saruga Shrine|1=Saruka Shrine}} * {{ill|Toyohira Shrine|ja|豊平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohira Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinano Shrine|ja|信濃神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Banknote Depiction == [[File:Early one yen banknote front and reverse.jpg|thumb|Front and reverse of an early one-yen banknote issued by the {{ill|Second National Bank of Japan|ja|第二国立銀行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second National Bank of Japan|1=Second National Bank of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11602278}}. The right of the front features Tamichi.]] {{main|National Bank Notes of Japan#One Yen Note}} In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 6), Tamichi was depicted on the one-yen convertible banknotes issued by the National Bank.<ref name="uemura">Uemura Takashi, ''Portraits on Banknotes in Modern History,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2015, pp. 66–67. ISBN 978-4-64-203845-4</ref>
Two theories exist regarding the design of the note: one suggests it depicts Kamitsukeno no Tamichi fending off a surprise attack by the Emishi, while the other posits it as depicting {{ill|Minamoto no Tametomo|en|Minamoto no Tametomo|qid=Q1339356|lt=Minamoto no Tametomo|1=Minamoto no Tametomo}} defending against Kudo Shigemitsu’s fleet. Notably, the figure is depicted with a disproportionately large head relative to the body, an unusual stylistic choice.<ref name="uemura"/>
== Legend == Legends about Tamichi are preserved at the {{ill|Saruka Shrine|en|Saruka Shrine|qid=Q3473746|lt=Saruga Shrine|1=Saruka Shrine}} in {{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|en|Hirakawa, Aomori|qid=Q695930|lt=Hirakawa, Aomori|1=Hirakawa, Aomori}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|qid=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}, and the {{ill|Toriya Shrine|ja|鳥屋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ishinomaki|en|Ishinomaki|qid=Q270814|lt=Ishinomaki|1=Ishinomaki}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|qid=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}.<ref>http://saruka.webcrow.jp/</ref><ref>https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010081</ref>
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tamichi was the son of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaratabe no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}} and the younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase|qq=}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 11, Emperor Nintoku’s 55th Year.
